# Poincaré Multimodal EHR Fusion 🧠⚕️

**Non-Euclidean Representation Learning for Hierarchical Clinical Diagnostics**

This repository contains the official implementation, training pipeline, and interactive Hospital Information System (HIS) deployment module for the paper: _"Poincaré Multimodal EHR Fusion: Non-Euclidean Representation Learning for Hierarchical Clinical Diagnostics"_.

## 🌟 Overview

Modern electronic health records (EHR) generate complex, multimodal data (unstructured clinical text + radiography imaging). Standard Euclidean deep learning models (![](data:image/png;base64,)) suffer from severe structural distortion when forced to map the hierarchical, tree-like taxonomy of medical diseases.

Our solution leverages **Hyperbolic Geometry (the Poincaré Ball model)** to explicitly preserve clinical hierarchies. Healthy patient profiles are embedded near the manifold's origin, while severe and overlapping pathologies expand radially toward the boundary.

### 🚀 Key Clinical Results (ChestMNIST N=22,433)

- **Mean Diagnostic AUROC:** 0.8967
- **Manifold Silhouette Score:** 0.3096
- **Dynamic Thresholding:** Achieved high screening sensitivity for critical/rare diseases (e.g., 0.96 Recall for Mass lesions, 0.92 for Infiltration).

## 🖥️ Interactive HIS Deployment Demo

We bridge the gap between theoretical geometry and clinical application with a standalone, client-side Web Application designed for modern Hospital Information Systems (HIS).

👉 [**Launch Live Interactive Demo**](#403zek6rzidg) _(Replace with your GitHub Pages link: https://\[username\].github.io/\[repo\]/)_

The dashboard includes three distinct views:

- **HIS Triage (Doctors):** A clinical queue that projects patient text and PACS imaging into 3D space, outputting priority screening alerts.
- **Manifold Explorer (Researchers):** A data science view to explore the procedural hyperbolic coordinates (![](data:image/png;base64,)) of the patient cohort.
- **Patient Report (Patients):** An accessible translation of the AI's geometric findings into actionable health insights.

_(The demo is built as a single index.html file using React, TailwindCSS, and Babel via CDN. No build step required-just host and run)._

## ⚙️ Architecture Core

- **Dual-Encoders:** ClinicalBERT for unstructured EHR notes and a CNN (ResNet) for Chest X-Rays.
- **Scaling Brake Stabilization:** A ![](data:image/png;base64,) scaling factor prevents the notorious "boundary mode collapse" typical in hyperbolic mapping.
- **Non-Euclidean Fusion:** Modalities are mapped via the exponential map (![](data:image/png;base64,)) and aggregated using **Möbius addition (![](data:image/png;base64,))**.
- **Riemannian Optimization:** Trained via geoopt.optim.RiemannianAdam.

## 🛠️ Installation & Training

### Prerequisites

- Python 3.9+
- PyTorch (CUDA recommended for full dataset training)

### Setup

\# Clone the repository  
git clone \[<https://github.com/yourusername/poincare-multimodal-fusion.git\>](<https://github.com/yourusername/poincare-multimodal-fusion.git>)  
cd poincare-multimodal-fusion  
<br/>\# Install dependencies  
pip install torch torchvision transformers geoopt medmnist scikit-learn pandas numpy matplotlib seaborn tqdm

### Running the Pipeline

To run the full end-to-end pipeline (Data Sourcing ![](data:image/png;base64,) BERT/CNN Extraction ![](data:image/png;base64,) Riemannian Training ![](data:image/png;base64,) 3D Rendering & Metric Evaluation):

python run_pipeline.py

_Outputs (Loss curves, 3D Manifold projections, and full_dataset_manifold.csv) will be saved to the /output_artifacts/ directory._

## 📄 Citation

If you find this code or methodology useful in your research, please consider citing our work:

@article{poincare_ehr_fusion_2024,  
title={Poincaré Multimodal EHR Fusion: Non-Euclidean Representation Learning for Hierarchical Clinical Diagnostics},  
author={Lead Researcher and Clinical Data Team},  
journal={Target: IEEE JBHI / MICCAI},  
year={2024}  
}

## 🤝 Contributing

Contributions to extend the manifold to other modalities (e.g., Genomic data, Time-series ECG) are welcome! Please open an issue or submit a pull request.
